rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC. VS. ELITE CONCORD GROUP LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Compel Responses To Pltfs Request For Production Of Documents; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR THURSDAY, APRIL 25, 2013 AT 9:00 AM, LINE 1, PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC.'S Motion To Compel Responses To PlAINtIFfs Request For Production Of Documents, And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Mr. Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DENIED AS UNTIMELY FILED (CCP SEC.2031.310(a)). DEFENDANTS DID FILE RESPONSES TO THE INITIAL DOCUMENT REQUEST WHICH, WHILE LARGELY CONSISTING OF OBJECTIONS, DID INCLUDE A WILLINGNESS TO ALLOW PLAINTIFF TO REVIEW AND COPY DOCUMENTS IN BANKERS BOXES. THE SUBSEQUENT CORRESPONDENCE CONTAINS NO REQUEST BY PLAINTIFF FOR AN EXTENSION OF TIME IN WHICH TO FILE A MOTION TO COMPEL IF IT DEEMED THE INITIAL RESPONSE TO BE INADEQUATE. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED AS IT APPEARS FROM THE CORRESPONDENCE THAT BOTH PARTIES WERE NOT AS COOPERATIVE AS ONE WOULD HAVE EXPECTED. FOR EXAMPLE THERE ARE NUMEROUS REQUESTS BY PLAINTIFF COMMENCING ON MAY 22, 2012 FOR DATES WHEN INSPECTION COULD BE MADE, ALL OF WHICH WERE UNANSWERED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Mr. Paul Renne

rulings.Case Title: JANNICKE KOLDERUP et al VS. OKLS, INC., DOING BUSINESS AS OKELL'S FIREPLACE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Olks, Inc. Doing Business As Okell'S Fireplace'S Motion To Compel Inspection And Testing Of Property; Request For An Award Of Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR THURSDAY, APRIL 25, 2013 AT 9:00 AM, LINE 4, Defendant Olks, Inc. Doing Business As Okell'S Fireplace'S Motion To Compel Inspection And Testing Of Property; Request For An Award Of Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Mr. Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DEFENDANT OKLS, INC. DBA OKELL'S FIREPLACE'S MOTION TO COMPEL INSPECTION AND TESTING OF PROPERTY IS DENIED. WHAT DEFENDANT IS ASKING THIS COURT TO DO IT NOT LIMITED TO "INSPECTION OR TESTING" OF PLAINTIFF'S PROPERTY. RATHER, DEFENDANT IN ITS MOTION SEEKS TO BE ALLOWED TO MAKE MODIFICATIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S PROPERTY. DEFENDANT'S CITE NO AUTHORITY WHICH WOULD AUTHORIZE A COURT UNDER THE PROVISIONS OF CCP SEC. 2031.010(C) TO COMPEL A PARTY TO ALLOW THE INSPECTING PARTY TO MAKE MODIFICATIONS TO THE OTHER PARTY'S PROPERTY. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Mr. Paul Renne

rulings.Case Title: ROBIN ECKERT MD VS. GUARDIAN LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO ORANGE COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 25, 2013, Line 6, DEFENDANT DAVE JONES' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Sustained with ten days leave to amend, if plaintiff is able to do so, to allege that the Insurance Commissioner breached a specific mandatory duty, which the complaint does not now do. (Schwartz v. Poizner (2010) 187 Cal. App. 4th 572). A writ of mandate cannot be based on general enforcement provisions or statutes involving the defendant's exercise of discretion. Plaintiff is also given ten days leave to amend, if plaintiff is able to do so, to allege facts showing that the defendant abused his discretion by acting arbitrarily. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ALEKSANDR SHOYKHET et al VS. TODD OPPENHEIMER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Enforcement Of The Subpoena For Ihss'S Records
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR THURSDAY, APRIL 25, 2013 AT 9:00 AM, LINE 3, DEFENDANT'S Motion To Compel Enforcement Of The Subpoena For Ihss'S Records. Pro Tem Judge Mr. Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS TODD OPPENHEIMER AND AHN OPPENHEIMER'S MOTION TO COMPEL ENFORCEMENT OF THE SUBPOENA FOR IHSS'S RECORDS OF PLAINTIFFS ALEKSANDR SHOYKHET AND LYUDMILA SHOYKHET AND THEIR SON VIKTORSHOYKHET IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT SHOW SUFFICIENT GOOD CAUSE AS TO WHY DISCOVERY SHOULD BE ALLOWED PAST THE DATE OF DISCOVERY CUT-OFF; THERE IS NO SHOWING THAT VIKTOR SHOYKHET RECEIVED THE REQUIRED NOTIFICATION THAT HIS RECORDS WERE BEING SOUGHT AND THE SCOPE OF THE SUBPOENA IS OVERLY BROAD. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE ASSSESSED. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER THE PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON APRIL 25, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Mr. Paul Renne

rulings.Case Title: CAREY CHIN et al VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, April 25, 2013, Line 18, CROSS COMPLAINANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO CROSS COMPLAINT Denied. Plaintiff has provided no authority requiring that a verified answer be made to the School District's Cross-Complaint. Code of Civil Procedure section 446 applies to public agencies as "plaintiffs." Although the terms "complaint"and "cross-complaint" are defined as interchangeable or inclusive in some parts of the Code of Civil Procedure (e.g., CCP 429.30 and CCP 438), Plaintiff has not cited to an applicable reference for section 446. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: LUIS SANCHEZ et al VS. LEONARD HO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, April 25, 2013, Line 21, CROSS COMPLAINANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO CROSS COMPLAINT Denied. Plaintiff has provided no authority requiring that verified answer be made to the School District's Cross-Complaint. Code of Civil Procedure section 446 applies to public agencies as "plaintiffs." Although the terms "complaint"and "cross-complaint" are defined as interchangeable or inclusive in some parts of the Code of Civil Procedure (e.g., CCP 429.30 and CCP 438), Plaintiff has not cited to an applicable reference for section 446. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ZEMACH HOLDINGS LLC, VS. ALAN CHAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgemnt On The Pleadings Per Ccp 438
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, April 25, 2013, Line 22, DEFENDANT ALAN CHAN, SPORTS TREND, INC.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Per CCP 438 Continued on the court's own motion to May 22, 2013 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than April 25, 2013. at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
